original_abstract: The purpose of this study was to identify the core knowledge and competencies needed for executive leadership in higher education administration as perceived by university presidents. Thirteen presidents shared the knowledge and competencies they perceived as being essential for an effective presidency. The respondents identified the important knowledge areas as: foundational; acquisition of cultural knowledge; and complex cognitive. They viewed personal attributes, management, and communications as the essential competency areas. The study found that presidents viewed themselves both as leaders and as managers.
humanized_abstract: The purpose of this study was to determine the core knowledge and competencies deemed essential by the executive leadership of higher education administration as identified by the chief executive officer of the institution, namely the university president. Thirteen university presidents identified the core knowledge and competencies they believed an executive leader of a higher education administrative unit would need in order to be effective in the role of the institution’s president. The presidents identified the following knowledge areas as core: foundational knowledge, cultural knowledge acquisition and advanced cognitive knowledge. In terms of competencies, the essential competencies were personal attributes, management, and communication. The chief executive officers of the institutions perceived the executive leader as a leader and a manager.
